title: BF-NEE Diagnostics
text:
This website provides exploratory analyses and diagnostic checks for the BF-NEE indicator.
The indicator links agricultural land expansion from LandSHIFT with ecosystem extent information from GlobES and attributes resulting ecosystem loss to commodity consumption.


The diagnostics focus on:

Global BF-NEE trends over time
Main drivers of the global footprint
Germany’s contribution to natural ecosystem displacement
Spatial hotspot analysis
Consistency checks between BF-NEE and GlobES ecosystem loss
Data quality and processing diagnostics






Country-level diagnostics and identification of outputs that were generated using outdated scaling assumptions.



Development of BF-NEE between 1995 and 2015.



Identification of countries contributing most strongly to global BF-NEE.



Identification of agricultural commodities contributing most strongly to ecosystem displacement.



Spatial distribution of BF-NEE and natural ecosystem loss.




Analyses currently use only countries that passed the processing and scale diagnostics.
Countries that still contain outputs generated prior to the cell-area correction are excluded until recalculated.

title: Methodological framework
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(tibble)
library(knitr)

title: Methodological framework
text:
Step 1: Remove excluded classes
The excluded classes are set to zero.
The remaining ecosystem shares are renormalized so that the non-excluded classes sum to one.

title: Methodological framework
text:
Step 2: Match agriculture to LandSHIFT
The total GlobES agricultural share is calculated as the sum of classes 14.1 and 15.8.
This agricultural share is replaced by the LandSHIFT agricultural share.

title: Methodological framework
text:
Step 3: Rescale remaining ecosystems
All non-agricultural ecosystem classes are rescaled so that the full ecosystem composition again sums to one.

title: Country drivers
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)



Attaching package: 'scales'


The following object is masked from 'package:readr':

    col_factor


Code
library(DT)

dir_diag <- "~/Symobio/Data/Processed/BF_NEE_Diagnostics"

diag <- read_csv(
  file.path(dir_diag, "bfnee_country_diagnostics_all_transitions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid <- read_csv(
  file.path(dir_diag, "bfnee_valid_countries.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid_iso3 <- valid %>%
  filter(valid_for_analysis) %>%
  pull(iso3)



Country contributions to global BF-NEE


Code
country_trend <- diag %>%
  filter(
    iso3 %in% valid_iso3,
    status == "ok",
    suspicious_old_scale == FALSE
  ) %>%
  group_by(iso3, year, transition) %>%
  summarise(
    bfnee_km2 = sum(bfnee_sum_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    natural_loss_km2 = sum(natural_loss_sum_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    weight_sum = mean(weight_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

global_by_year <- country_trend %>%
  group_by(year, transition) %>%
  summarise(
    global_bfnee_km2 = sum(bfnee_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

country_trend <- country_trend %>%
  left_join(global_by_year, by = c("year", "transition")) %>%
  mutate(
    share_global_bfnee = bfnee_km2 / global_bfnee_km2
  )




Top countries by period


Code
top_countries_by_period <- country_trend %>%
  group_by(year, transition) %>%
  slice_max(bfnee_km2, n = 15, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(
  top_countries_by_period,
  aes(
    x = reorder(iso3, bfnee_km2),
    y = bfnee_km2
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ transition, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Top BF-NEE countries by transition",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Country",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Top countries in latest period


Code
latest_year <- max(country_trend$year, na.rm = TRUE)

top_latest <- country_trend %>%
  filter(year == latest_year) %>%
  arrange(desc(bfnee_km2)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(
  top_latest,
  aes(
    x = reorder(iso3, bfnee_km2),
    y = bfnee_km2
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Top BF-NEE countries in", latest_year),
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Country",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Share of global BF-NEE


Code
ggplot(
  top_latest,
  aes(
    x = reorder(iso3, share_global_bfnee),
    y = share_global_bfnee
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Share of global BF-NEE in", latest_year),
    subtitle = "Top 20 countries, validated countries only",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Share of global BF-NEE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Country trajectories


Code
top_overall <- country_trend %>%
  group_by(iso3) %>%
  summarise(
    total_bfnee_km2 = sum(bfnee_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  slice_max(total_bfnee_km2, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(iso3)

ggplot(
  country_trend %>% filter(iso3 %in% top_overall),
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = bfnee_km2,
    group = iso3,
    linetype = iso3
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "BF-NEE trajectories for top countries",
    subtitle = "Top 10 countries by total BF-NEE across all periods",
    x = "Year",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)",
    linetype = "Country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Diagnostic table


Code
datatable(
  country_trend %>%
    arrange(desc(bfnee_km2)),
  options = list(pageLength = 25)
)

title: Global BF-NEE trends
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)



Attaching package: 'scales'


The following object is masked from 'package:readr':

    col_factor


Code
dir_diag <- "~/Symobio/Data/Processed/BF_NEE_Diagnostics"

diag <- read_csv(
  file.path(
    dir_diag,
    "bfnee_country_diagnostics_all_transitions.csv"
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid <- read_csv(
  file.path(
    dir_diag,
    "bfnee_valid_countries.csv"
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid_iso3 <- valid %>%
  filter(valid_for_analysis) %>%
  pull(iso3)



Overview
This page summarizes global BF-NEE trends based on countries that passed all processing and scale diagnostics.


Code
global_trend <- diag %>%
  filter(
    iso3 %in% valid_iso3,
    status == "ok",
    suspicious_old_scale == FALSE
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, transition) %>%
  summarise(
    global_bfnee_km2 =
      sum(bfnee_sum_km2, na.rm = TRUE),

    global_natural_loss_km2 =
      sum(natural_loss_sum_km2, na.rm = TRUE),

    n_countries =
      n_distinct(iso3),

    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  mutate(
    bfnee_index =
      global_bfnee_km2 /
      first(global_bfnee_km2) * 100,

    natural_loss_index =
      global_natural_loss_km2 /
      first(global_natural_loss_km2) * 100,

    bfnee_share_of_loss =
      global_bfnee_km2 /
      global_natural_loss_km2
  )




Global BF-NEE


Code
ggplot(
  global_trend,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = global_bfnee_km2
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Global BF-NEE",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Year",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Global natural ecosystem loss


Code
ggplot(
  global_trend,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = global_natural_loss_km2
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Global natural ecosystem loss",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Natural ecosystem loss (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Absolute comparison


Code
global_trend_long <- global_trend %>%
  select(
    year,
    global_bfnee_km2,
    global_natural_loss_km2
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      global_bfnee_km2,
      global_natural_loss_km2
    ),
    names_to = "indicator",
    values_to = "value_km2"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    indicator = recode(
      indicator,
      global_bfnee_km2 =
        "BF-NEE",
      global_natural_loss_km2 =
        "Natural ecosystem loss"
    )
  )




Code
ggplot(
  global_trend_long,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = value_km2,
    linetype = indicator
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Absolute comparison",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Area (km²)",
    linetype = "Indicator"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Relative change (first period = 100)
This figure compares rates of change only.
Both indicators are normalized so that the first period equals 100.


Code
global_trend_index_long <- global_trend %>%
  select(
    year,
    bfnee_index,
    natural_loss_index
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      bfnee_index,
      natural_loss_index
    ),
    names_to = "indicator",
    values_to = "index"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    indicator = recode(
      indicator,
      bfnee_index =
        "BF-NEE",
      natural_loss_index =
        "Natural ecosystem loss"
    )
  )




Code
ggplot(
  global_trend_index_long,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = index,
    linetype = indicator
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Relative change in BF-NEE and natural ecosystem loss",
    subtitle = "First period = 100",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Index",
    linetype = "Indicator"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











BF-NEE share of natural ecosystem loss
This figure shows the proportion of natural ecosystem loss represented by BF-NEE.


Code
ggplot(
  global_trend,
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = bfnee_share_of_loss
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(
      accuracy = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "BF-NEE as share of natural ecosystem loss",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Year",
    y = "BF-NEE / natural ecosystem loss"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Diagnostic table


Code
global_trend


# A tibble: 4 × 8

1  2000 1995_2000            83960.                7667465.         153
2  2005 2000_2005            49848.                3900626.         153

title: Regional regressions
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
library(DT)
library(scales)



Attaching package: 'scales'


The following object is masked from 'package:readr':

    col_factor


Code
df <- read_csv(
  "data/bfnee_regression_dataset.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

df_model <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(continent),
    !is.na(log_bfnee),
    !is.na(log_natural_loss),
    !is.na(log_weight_sum),
    natural_loss_sum_km2 > 0,
    bfnee_sum_km2 > 0
  )



Data coverage


Code
df_model %>%
  distinct(continent, iso3) %>%
  count(continent, name = "n_countries") %>%
  arrange(continent)


# A tibble: 6 × 2
  continent     n_countries
  <chr>               <int>
1 Africa                 41
2 Asia                   42
3 Europe                 32
4 North America          14
5 Oceania                 2
6 South America           9




Code
df_model %>%
  count(continent, year) %>%
  arrange(continent, year)


# A tibble: 24 × 3
   continent  year     n
   <chr>     <dbl> <int>
 1 Africa     2000    41
 2 Africa     2005    41
 3 Africa     2010    41
 4 Africa     2015    41
 5 Asia       2000    42
 6 Asia       2005    42
 7 Asia       2010    41
 8 Asia       2015    41
 9 Europe     2000    32
10 Europe     2005    32
# ℹ 14 more rows




Model specification
The first regional consistency model is:
log(BF-NEE + 1) ~ log(natural ecosystem loss + 1) + log(weight sum + 1)
where weight_sum represents the aggregated commodity-consumption and production-attribution component.


BF-NEE and natural ecosystem loss


Code
ggplot(
  df_model,
  aes(
    x = natural_loss_sum_km2,
    y = bfnee_sum_km2
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ continent, scales = "free") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "BF-NEE vs natural ecosystem loss",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Natural ecosystem loss (km²)",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'











Log-log relationship


Code
ggplot(
  df_model,
  aes(
    x = log_natural_loss,
    y = log_bfnee
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ continent, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Log-log relationship between BF-NEE and natural ecosystem loss",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "log(natural ecosystem loss + 1)",
    y = "log(BF-NEE + 1)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'











Regional regression models


Code
fit_region <- function(x) {
  lm(
    log_bfnee ~ log_natural_loss + log_weight_sum,
    data = x
  )
}

regional_models <- df_model %>%
  group_by(continent) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    model <- fit_region(.x)
    
    tidy_model <- tidy(model)
    glance_model <- glance(model)
    
    tidy_model %>%
      mutate(
        r_squared = glance_model$r.squared,
        adj_r_squared = glance_model$adj.r.squared,
        n_obs = glance_model$nobs
      )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

regional_models


# A tibble: 18 × 9
   continent term  estimate std.error statistic  p.value r_squared adj_r_squared
   <chr>     <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl>
 1 Africa    (Int…   -5.80     0.383     -15.1  8.61e-33     0.813         0.811
 2 Africa    log_…    0.951    0.0395     24.1  2.97e-55     0.813         0.811
 3 Africa    log_…  107.       6.92       15.5  7.85e-34     0.813         0.811
 4 Asia      (Int…   -4.32     0.231     -18.7  2.32e-42     0.870         0.869
 5 Asia      log_…    0.851    0.0263     32.4  6.60e-73     0.870         0.869
 6 Asia      log_…   53.0      4.05       13.1  3.66e-27     0.870         0.869
 7 Europe    (Int…   -2.33     0.272      -8.56 3.51e-14     0.856         0.853
 8 Europe    log_…    0.815    0.0341     23.9  1.81e-48     0.856         0.853
 9 Europe    log_…    9.48     0.587      16.1  2.21e-32     0.856         0.853
10 North Am… (Int…   -4.58     0.262     -17.5  1.20e-23     0.955         0.953
11 North Am… log_…    0.912    0.0293     31.1  9.94e-36     0.955         0.953
12 North Am… log_…   56.6      3.77       15.0  9.39e-21     0.955         0.953
13 Oceania   (Int…   -5.80     0.471     -12.3  6.20e- 5     0.992         0.989
14 Oceania   log_…    1.04     0.0423     24.5  2.13e- 6     0.992         0.989
15 Oceania   log_…   69.8      8.35        8.37 3.99e- 4     0.992         0.989
16 South Am… (Int…   -6.20     0.764      -8.13 2.23e- 9     0.900         0.894
17 South Am… log_…    1.08     0.0705     15.4  1.35e-16     0.900         0.894
18 South Am… log_…   52.3      5.71        9.15 1.41e-10     0.900         0.894
# ℹ 1 more variable: n_obs <int>




Code
datatable(
  regional_models %>%
    arrange(continent, term),
  options = list(pageLength = 25)
)








Coefficient comparison


Code
regional_coefficients <- regional_models %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

ggplot(
  regional_coefficients,
  aes(
    x = continent,
    y = estimate
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
      ymax = estimate + 1.96 * std.error
    ),
    width = 0.15
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ term, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Regional regression coefficients",
    subtitle = "Error bars show approximate 95% confidence intervals",
    x = "Region",
    y = "Coefficient estimate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )











Model fit by region


Code
model_fit <- regional_models %>%
  distinct(
    continent,
    r_squared,
    adj_r_squared,
    n_obs
  )

ggplot(
  model_fit,
  aes(
    x = reorder(continent, r_squared),
    y = r_squared
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Model fit by region",
    x = "Region",
    y = "R²"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Residual diagnostics


Code
regional_augmented <- df_model %>%
  group_by(continent) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    model <- fit_region(.x)
    augment(model, data = .x)
  }) %>%
  ungroup()




Code
ggplot(
  regional_augmented,
  aes(
    x = .fitted,
    y = .resid
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ continent, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Residual diagnostics by region",
    x = "Fitted log(BF-NEE + 1)",
    y = "Residual"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Largest positive residuals
Countries with large positive residuals have higher BF-NEE than expected from natural ecosystem loss and the aggregate attribution weight.


Code
regional_augmented %>%
  arrange(desc(.resid)) %>%
  select(
    continent,
    iso3,
    country_name,
    transition,
    year,
    bfnee_sum_km2,
    natural_loss_sum_km2,
    weight_sum,
    .fitted,
    .resid
  ) %>%
  head(30) %>%
  datatable(
    options = list(pageLength = 15)
  )








Largest negative residuals
Countries with large negative residuals have lower BF-NEE than expected from natural ecosystem loss and the aggregate attribution weight.


Code
regional_augmented %>%
  arrange(.resid) %>%
  select(
    continent,
    iso3,
    country_name,
    transition,
    year,
    bfnee_sum_km2,
    natural_loss_sum_km2,
    weight_sum,
    .fitted,
    .resid
  ) %>%
  head(30) %>%
  datatable(
    options = list(pageLength = 15)
  )

title: Commodity drivers
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)



Attaching package: 'scales'


The following object is masked from 'package:readr':

    col_factor


Code
library(DT)

dir_diag <- "~/Symobio/Data/Processed/BF_NEE_Diagnostics"
dir_bf <- "~/Symobio/Data/Processed/BF_NEE_Countries"

valid <- read_csv(
  file.path(dir_diag, "bfnee_valid_countries.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid_iso3 <- valid %>%
  filter(valid_for_analysis) %>%
  pull(iso3)



Load commodity-level BF-NEE


Code
read_commodity_bf <- function(iso3) {
  
  iso3_lower <- tolower(iso3)
  dir_country <- file.path(dir_bf, iso3)
  
  files <- list.files(
    dir_country,
    pattern = paste0("^BF_NEE_by_commodity_", iso3_lower, "_.*\\.csv$"),
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  if (length(files) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  bind_rows(lapply(files, function(f) {
    x <- read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE)
    x$source_file <- basename(f)
    x
  }))
}

commodity_raw <- bind_rows(
  lapply(valid_iso3, read_commodity_bf)
)

commodity_bf <- commodity_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(BF_NEE_commodity))




Global commodity contributions


Code
commodity_global <- commodity_bf %>%
  group_by(year, transition, commodity) %>%
  summarise(
    bfnee_km2 = sum(BF_NEE_commodity, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

commodity_global_total <- commodity_global %>%
  group_by(year, transition) %>%
  summarise(
    global_bfnee_km2 = sum(bfnee_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

commodity_global <- commodity_global %>%
  left_join(
    commodity_global_total,
    by = c("year", "transition")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share_global_bfnee = bfnee_km2 / global_bfnee_km2
  )




Top commodities by period


Code
top_commodities_by_period <- commodity_global %>%
  group_by(year, transition) %>%
  slice_max(bfnee_km2, n = 15, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(
  top_commodities_by_period,
  aes(
    x = reorder(commodity, bfnee_km2),
    y = bfnee_km2
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ transition, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Top commodities by BF-NEE and transition",
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Commodity",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Top commodities in latest period


Code
latest_year <- max(commodity_global$year, na.rm = TRUE)

top_latest <- commodity_global %>%
  filter(year == latest_year) %>%
  arrange(desc(bfnee_km2)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(
  top_latest,
  aes(
    x = reorder(commodity, bfnee_km2),
    y = bfnee_km2
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Top commodities in", latest_year),
    subtitle = "Validated countries only",
    x = "Commodity",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Commodity shares of global BF-NEE


Code
ggplot(
  top_latest,
  aes(
    x = reorder(commodity, share_global_bfnee),
    y = share_global_bfnee
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Commodity share of global BF-NEE in", latest_year),
    subtitle = "Top 20 commodities, validated countries only",
    x = "Commodity",
    y = "Share of global BF-NEE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Commodity trajectories


Code
top_overall <- commodity_global %>%
  group_by(commodity) %>%
  summarise(
    total_bfnee_km2 = sum(bfnee_km2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  slice_max(total_bfnee_km2, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(commodity)

ggplot(
  commodity_global %>% filter(commodity %in% top_overall),
  aes(
    x = year,
    y = bfnee_km2,
    group = commodity,
    linetype = commodity
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "BF-NEE trajectories for top commodities",
    subtitle = "Top 10 commodities by total BF-NEE across all periods",
    x = "Year",
    y = "BF-NEE (km²)",
    linetype = "Commodity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Diagnostic table


Code
datatable(
  commodity_global %>%
    arrange(desc(bfnee_km2)),
  options = list(pageLength = 25)
)

title: Processing status and scale diagnostics
text:
Code
library(dplyr)



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union


Code
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)

dir_diag <- "~/Symobio/Data/Processed/BF_NEE_Diagnostics"

diag <- read_csv(
  file.path(dir_diag, "bfnee_country_diagnostics_all_transitions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

valid <- read_csv(
  file.path(dir_diag, "bfnee_valid_countries.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)



Summary


Code
valid %>%
  count(valid_for_analysis)


# A tibble: 1 × 2
  valid_for_analysis     n
  <lgl>              <int>
1 TRUE                 153




Code
ggplot(valid, aes(x = valid_for_analysis)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Countries passing BF-NEE diagnostics",
    x = "Valid for analysis",
    y = "Number of countries"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Old-scale outputs by transition


Code
scale_summary <- diag %>%
  count(transition, suspicious_old_scale)

scale_summary


# A tibble: 4 × 3
  transition suspicious_old_scale     n
  <chr>      <lgl>                <int>
1 1995_2000  FALSE                  153
2 2000_2005  FALSE                  153
3 2005_2010  FALSE                  153
4 2010_2015  FALSE                  153




Code
ggplot(scale_summary, aes(x = transition, y = n, fill = suspicious_old_scale)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Suspicious old-scale outputs by transition",
    x = "Transition",
    y = "Number of country-transition outputs",
    fill = "Old scale?"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()











Countries excluded from analysis


Code
excluded <- valid %>%
  filter(!valid_for_analysis)

datatable(excluded)








Diagnostic table


Code
datatable(
  diag %>%
    arrange(desc(suspicious_old_scale), iso3, transition),
  options = list(pageLength = 25)
)
